title: MoQ broadcast — lazy fan-out, blind relay, metadata-only admission (the abuse lever)
status: characterized (relay LOGIC green; full moq-rs/GStreamer + transport-carried deferred)
harness: crates/moq-lazy-spike — deterministic in-process publisher/relay/subscriber; ChaCha20-Poly1305 opaque frames. Proves the relay LOGIC, not the codec/transport (those are de-risked by n0's iroh-live + the proven iroh fabric).

lazy_fan_out.detail: publisher egress = 0 across 100 produce-ticks with 0 subscribers; produces the instant one subscriber attaches. 'Nothing to fan out if nobody is watching' — the battery/compute/privacy win, demonstrated.

fanout_cost_linear.detail: relay egress = frames * N exactly; zero when unwatched

relay_blind.detail: relay forwards opaque encrypted frames, payload_keys_held=0; every subscriber decrypts locally (end-to-end blindness, media analog of the message blind broker)

metadata_admission_abuse_lever.detail: max-audience cap (5) refuses 3 of 8 join attempts; members-only refuses 1 non-member of 3 — all from subscribe METADATA alone, reading zero frame bytes. The lever against piracy/abuse (the Rave trap) is scale + peer restriction, NOT content inspection.

verdict: characterized: the Croft-novel MoQ relay properties — lazy (nothing until watched), blind (no payload key), and an abuse lever that is scale + peer restriction enforced from metadata alone — all hold. The full codec/transport form is meer role P5: moq-rs/iroh-live carried over the iroh fabric E9/E10 proved.
open_edges: Relay logic only — not real moq-rs Tracks, GStreamer/Opus encoding, or WebTransport browser reach (the design calls these 'mostly assembly, not build', de-risked by n0's iroh-live).
In-process simulation, not transport-carried; the wire form is meer P5 over iroh (E9/E10 proven).
Constant-bitrate/padding to defeat per-stream bitrate inference (TC-META2) and the full AR-4-for-media bound (TC-META1) are separate metadata-cost measurements, not run here.
